subject: Love the product — small idea for the inbox view
body:
Hey team!

First off, I've been using your tool for about 4 months now and it's genuinely changed how my support team operates. Thank you for what you build. 🙏

One small thing — would it be possible to add a 'recently viewed tickets' section to the inbox sidebar? I bounce between 5-6 tickets at a time and the back-and-forth between search results gets clicky.

No pressure, just thought I'd flop the idea over.

Aarav

subject: Feature idea: Slack thread → ticket conversion
body:
Hi,

Love the Slack integration. One workflow gap: when our team discusses an issue in a Slack thread and decides it should become a ticket, we currently copy/paste the whole thread manually.

Would be magical if there was a slash command (/triage or similar) that turned the active Slack thread into a ticket here, with all messages as context.

Thanks for considering!

Priya

subject: tiny request from a happy customer 🚀
body:
hey 👋

building my whole company on this. real talk. just one thing — can we get a dark mode for the embedded chat widget? our dashboard is dark themed and the white widget pops weirdly against it.

keep cooking 💯

chen

subject: Saved search / smart filters?
body:
Hi team,

I use the same 3-4 filter combinations every day (e.g., 'urgent + assigned to me + open' and 'billing tag + last 7 days'). Would be amazing if these could be saved as named smart views in the sidebar.

Would save me 30 seconds × 20 times a day = real time.

Sofia

subject: Integration idea: Linear (we'd use it heavily)
body:
Hi,

We're heavy Linear users and I keep wishing for a direct link between tickets here and Linear issues. Specifically:
- Create Linear issue from a ticket with one click
- Sync status both ways
- Show ticket reference inside Linear

If this is on the roadmap, I'd love to be in the beta. Happy to share our workflow context.

Kwame

subject: I love your product BUT...
body:
Hey,

I love your product — like genuinely, it's my favorite SaaS tool we use. BUT — the mobile experience is kinda rough. I get notifications, tap them, and the deep-link doesn't take me to the right ticket. I end up scrolling the inbox manually on a 5-inch screen.

Any chance of mobile getting some love? Not a complaint, just a love letter with a wish.

Elena

subject: Bulk action improvement
body:
Hi team,

When I select 30+ tickets to bulk-tag, the action takes 12-15 seconds and there's no progress indicator. Couple times I've thought it crashed and reloaded.

Would be helpful if (a) the bulk action was faster, or (b) at minimum there was a 'processing X of Y' indicator.

Diego

subject: Massive thanks + one ask
body:
Hi!

I just wanted to say — I switched from Zendesk to your product 6 weeks ago and my team's morale has noticeably improved. The UI is just so much less hostile.

One ask: any plan for a public roadmap? It would help us understand what's coming and plan our own work around it.

Ananya

subject: Feature idea for exports
body:
Hi,

Quick idea — could the CSV export include a 'filtered by' header row so when I send the file to teammates, they can see what filters were applied? Right now I have to write that in the email.

Merci!

Jean

subject: Wishlist (in priority order)
body:
Hi team,

I use the tool daily and have been mentally collecting these. In rough priority order:

1. Inline ticket editing from the list view (high — saves so many clicks)
2. Custom keyboard shortcuts (medium — power user nice-to-have)
3. Mention notifications in the dashboard, not just email (medium)
4. Ticket templates for repeated reply patterns (low — but lovely)
5. Public API for our internal Slack bot (low — we'd build it ourselves)

Thanks for reading!

Li Na

subject: shipping fast & loving it — small ask
body:
y'all are shipping at insane speed. respect 🫡

one thing: webhook signatures pls. we want to verify incoming webhooks but you don't sign them yet. blocking us from using webhooks in prod.

mateo

subject: Suggestion for the analytics page
body:
Hello,

The analytics page is great but I find myself wanting to compare two time windows side by side (e.g., 'this week vs last week'). Currently I have to take screenshots and compare manually.

A comparison toggle would make it 10x more useful for me. Would also love trend lines on the totals.

Thanks!
Amina

subject: Recurring tickets feature?
body:
Hi,

We have certain ticket types that recur on a schedule (weekly customer check-ins, monthly compliance reviews). Right now I create them manually each time.

Would a 'recurring ticket template' feature be feasible? Set frequency, template body, default assignee, and it auto-creates on schedule.

Would save my team a real chunk of time.

Oliver

subject: Idea: 'similar tickets' sidebar
body:
Hi team!

When I open a ticket, I'd love a sidebar that shows 3-5 similar past tickets (based on content / tags / sender). Often the answer is something we've already solved before, and finding it manually is the slowest part of my day.

Bet your team has the data for this. Worth a thought!

Riya

subject: Saved views request
body:
Hi,

Would it be possible to have personal saved views that don't get shared across the team? Right now every saved view is global. Some of my views are just for me (e.g., 'tickets I'm CC'd on that I haven't read') and I don't need to pollute the team list.

Tomas

subject: Small UX win: confirmation when archiving
body:
Hi,

I archived a ticket by accident last week and it took me 10 minutes to find it again because there's no toast / undo. Could you add a 5-second 'Undo' toast after archive actions? Same pattern Gmail uses.

Mei

subject: Feature: schedule a reply for later
body:
Hi team,

One workflow gap — sometimes I draft a reply at 9 PM but don't want to send it then (looks bad for boundaries / wakes the customer up). Could we have a 'send later' option like Gmail's scheduled send?

Would genuinely improve life quality.

Carlos

subject: AI summary on long threads — yes please
body:
Hi,

Long customer threads (15+ messages) are painful to read top-to-bottom when someone hands off to me. An AI-generated summary at the top of the thread (1-2 sentences: 'customer reported X on Mar 5, resolved Y on Mar 8, currently asking about Z') would be a game-changer.

I saw you're shipping AI features in the changelog — adding my vote here.

Nandini

subject: Just here to say thanks
body:
Hi,

No feature request today, just wanted to drop a line saying your product has made a real difference for our small team. The onboarding was painless and the docs are some of the best I've used.

Keep building.

Pablo

subject: Custom fields with conditional logic?
body:
Hi,

A more advanced ask — would it be possible to have custom fields that show conditionally? Example: if a ticket is tagged 'refund', show fields for amount + payment method. If tagged 'technical', show fields for browser + URL.

Would reduce noise on simple tickets and capture better structured data on complex ones.

Anna

subject: Internal notes formatting
body:
Hi!

Internal notes (the @mention ones) currently support markdown — beautiful. But could they support code blocks with syntax highlighting? Our team often shares snippets of customer-facing API calls and right now they render as plain text.

Thanks!
Rahul

subject: Dark mode in the changelog email?
body:
Hi,

Minor nitpick — your changelog emails are pure white background. I read email in dark mode and the contrast is jarring. Could you add a dark variant? Most newsletter tools support this natively now.

Eva

subject: Power user request: command palette
body:
Hey team,

I'm in the tool every day, all day. The single feature that would transform my experience: a Cmd+K command palette. Linear/Raycast-style. Search tickets, jump to settings, perform actions, all from keyboard.

If you build it I will sing your praises in every SaaS slack I'm in.

Samuel

subject: Could we tag tickets from email replies?
body:
Hi,

Quick idea — when a customer replies via email, sometimes I just want to add a tag without opening the dashboard. Could we add tags via email reply? E.g., responding with '+tag billing' on its own line adds the tag.

Not urgent, just a thought from someone who lives in their inbox.

Isabella

subject: love the new look 🎨 + question
body:
the redesign last month is gorgeous. genuinely the cleanest support tool ui i've ever used.

q: any plan to expose the design system as a public CSS file or figma? we'd love to keep our embedded chat widget visually consistent with our app.

arjun

subject: Suggestion: timezone display on tickets
body:
Hi,

My team is distributed across 5 timezones. When I see 'Replied at 3:42 PM' on a ticket, I don't know if that means the customer's local time or mine. Could timestamps optionally show with timezone (e.g., '3:42 PM CET')?

Lucia

subject: Webhook for ticket status change
body:
Hi,

We have a webhook for 'ticket created' but not for 'ticket status changed.' We'd love the latter — would let us auto-update our customer-facing status page when tickets resolve.

Thanks for considering!
Wei

subject: Snippets / canned responses
body:
Hi,

Do you support snippets / canned responses? I have about 15 stock replies I send daily ('here are the steps to reset', 'pricing question - here's the doc', etc.). Right now I keep them in a Notion page and copy-paste.

If this exists and I missed it — point me to it. If it doesn't — please build it!

Fatima

subject: Multi-language support for customer-facing emails
body:
Hi,

We have customers in Poland, Germany, and Spain. We currently respond in English regardless because we don't have an easy translation workflow. Would be amazing if the compose window had a 'translate to customer's language' option (based on browser locale or a manual override).

Probably not trivial to build but flagging the need.

Marek

subject: Quick win: keyboard nav for the inbox list
body:
Hi team,

Love the tool. One small thing — when I'm in the inbox list, j/k for next/previous (Gmail style) would make scanning so much faster. Currently I have to click each one.

Neha

subject: Idea: customer journey on profile
body:
Hi,

When I open a customer profile, I see their recent tickets. Would love to also see their journey — when they signed up, plan changes, key product events. Helps me answer their question with full context.

I know you have this data via the integrations. Surfacing it would be magic.

Miguel

subject: Compliments + tiny ask
body:
Hi!

First time writing in — three months of using the tool and it's solid. Onboarding was the smoothest I've experienced.

Tiny ask: could the favicon turn red when there's an unread urgent ticket? Helps me notice without having the tab in focus.

Chinwe

subject: API endpoint for analytics?
body:
Bonjour,

We pull data from your dashboard manually into our weekly reporting deck. Would be lovely if there was a public API endpoint for the same analytics data (response times, ticket volume, CSAT). Anything we can grab programmatically?

Merci,
Pierre

subject: Two ideas from a daily user
body:
Hi,

Love the tool. Two ideas while they're fresh:

1. Bulk merge tickets — currently I can merge 2 at a time. Sometimes I have 4-5 duplicates.
2. 'Resolved this week' digest email every Friday — quick win-list for my team's Monday standup.

Kavya

subject: Idea: dropdown for common assignees
body:
Hi,

When I assign a ticket, the dropdown lists ALL team members alphabetically. In practice, 90% of my assignments go to 3 people. Could the dropdown show 'frequent assignees' at the top, then alphabetical below?

Not a blocker, just friction.

Javier

subject: Notion integration — please??
body:
Hi,

We document everything in Notion. Would love an integration where I can link a ticket to a Notion page (e.g., the internal runbook for an issue type) and have the link surface inline.

Grace

subject: Suggestion: SLA countdown in the ticket header
body:
Hi,

We have SLA-bound tickets and would love a visible countdown timer in the ticket header (e.g., '4h 12m remaining' for an SLA-tagged P1). Right now I have to mentally calculate this against the timestamp.

Viktor

subject: love it. small thing about the search bar
body:
the global search bar is great but it only searches subjects. could it also search body content? sometimes i remember a phrase the customer used but not their subject line.

thanks 🙏
sneha

subject: Read receipts on internal notes?
body:
Hi,

When I @mention a teammate on an internal note, I don't know if they've seen it. Could you add a small 'seen by' indicator like Slack does?

Would reduce the 'hey did you see my note' pings.

Ricardo

subject: feature req from a heavy api user
body:
hey,

built a slack bot on top of your API. one missing piece: pagination headers. when i fetch /tickets, i get items but no easy way to know if there's a next page. having to count results vs limit is fragile.

add Link header (RFC 5988) or similar pls.

zhang min

subject: Loving the product! Tiny copy nit
body:
Hi team,

The empty state on the inbox says 'You have no tickets — enjoy the peace!' which is delightful. But the loading state right before is 'Loading...' which feels jarring after.

Could the loading state get some of that personality too? Even just 'Fetching your inbox...' would feel more on-brand.

Aditya

subject: Group assignments — round robin?
body:
Hi,

When a ticket is assigned to a team (not an individual), would it be possible to round-robin auto-assign to a specific person? Right now it just sits unassigned to anyone in particular and no one picks it up quickly.

Helena

subject: Suggestion: customer tagging from email signature parsing
body:
Hi,

A lot of incoming emails have signatures with the customer's company / role. Right now those are unstructured. Could you parse signatures and surface company / role as a tag automatically?

Would save us manual tagging time.

Emeka

subject: Three months in — feedback
body:
Hi team,

We've been on the platform for 3 months now. Quick honest feedback:

What's working great: the dashboard, response speed, integrations.
What we miss from our old tool: a 'parking lot' status for tickets we want to come back to but don't want cluttering the open queue.

Something like 'snoozed' or 'parked' with a return date.

Laura

subject: Could we get a public roadmap?
body:
Hi,

Love the product and the velocity. Only thing I'd love more is a public roadmap so we can plan our own work around what's coming.

Even 'this quarter / next quarter / later' buckets would help.

Rohan

subject: Mention-triggered Slack alerts
body:
Hi,

When I'm @mentioned on a ticket, I currently get an email. Could there be an option to also send a Slack DM? Email is a lot slower for me than Slack.

Thanks!
Beatriz

subject: Idea: customer satisfaction survey trigger
body:
Hi,

We currently send CSAT surveys manually after resolution. Could there be an automated trigger (e.g., 24h after a ticket is resolved, send a 1-click satisfaction survey)?

Would let us track CSAT trends without manual work.

Yusuf

subject: Enterprise security features
body:
Hi,

As we grow, our security team is asking about features I don't see yet:
- IP allowlisting
- Session timeout configuration per role
- Audit log export

Are any of these planned? Happy to share more about what our security team needs if helpful.

Clara

subject: Inline previews for shared links
body:
Hi,

When a customer pastes a URL in their message, it shows as raw text. Would be nicer if it expanded inline (OG image + title) like Slack does. Helps me grok context faster without leaving the ticket.

Lukas

subject: Just signed up — first impressions = love
body:
Hi team!

Signed up yesterday and migrated our entire support workflow over today. The onboarding flow was the smoothest I've ever seen.

One small ask: it would be helpful if there was a 'sample data' toggle for new accounts so we could see what a populated dashboard looks like before importing our own tickets.

Thanks for building something great.

Mariana
